questions.q: Whose personal maidservant was Manthara?
questions.explanation: Manthara was the trusted hunchbacked maidservant of Queen Kaikeyi.

questions.q: What distinctive physical feature is Manthara traditionally described as having?
questions.explanation: Manthara is famously described as a hunchback, which is why she is often called the crooked maid.

questions.q: Against whose coronation did Manthara incite Kaikeyi?
questions.explanation: Manthara turned Kaikeyi against the planned coronation of Rama as crown prince of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Whom did Manthara want to be made king in Rama's place?
questions.explanation: Manthara schemed to have Bharata, Kaikeyi's own son, crowned instead of Rama.

questions.q: In which city did Manthara live and carry out her scheming?
questions.explanation: Manthara lived in the royal palace of Ayodhya, the capital of King Dasharatha's kingdom.

questions.q: What news first alarmed Manthara and set her scheme in motion?
options: The wedding of Rama and Sita
questions.explanation: Manthara grew alarmed upon learning that Ayodhya was being decorated for Rama's imminent coronation.

questions.q: How did Kaikeyi initially react when Manthara first told her of Rama's coronation?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi was at first delighted by the news, even rewarding Manthara, before being poisoned against it.

questions.q: Manthara is best remembered in the Ramayana as a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: Manthara has become a byword for the malicious adviser whose poisonous counsel brings ruin.

questions.q: What was the ultimate consequence of Manthara's scheme for Rama?
questions.explanation: Manthara's counsel led Kaikeyi to demand Rama's fourteen-year exile to the forest.

questions.q: Manthara's plotting takes place in which book (kanda) of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Manthara's intrigue unfolds in the Ayodhya Kanda, which centers on events in the city of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Manthara reminded Kaikeyi of two boons that the king had once promised her. Who had granted those boons?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha had earlier promised Kaikeyi two boons, which Manthara urged her to invoke.

questions.q: What two demands did Manthara persuade Kaikeyi to make using the boons?
questions.explanation: Manthara's plan was for Kaikeyi to demand Bharata be made king and Rama be sent into exile.

questions.q: How long an exile did Manthara have Kaikeyi demand for Rama?
questions.explanation: At Manthara's urging, Kaikeyi demanded that Rama be banished to the forest for fourteen years.

questions.q: Into which room or chamber did Kaikeyi retreat after Manthara convinced her?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi withdrew into the krodhagara, the chamber of anger, where Dasharatha found her in distress.

questions.q: Which argument did Manthara use to frighten Kaikeyi about her own future?
options: That Kausalya would become supreme queen and Kaikeyi would be reduced to servitude
questions.explanation: Manthara warned that with Rama as king, his mother Kausalya would dominate and Kaikeyi would be made a mere servant.

questions.q: Where was Bharata when Manthara's scheme was being set in motion?
options: Away at his maternal uncle's kingdom
questions.explanation: Bharata was away visiting his maternal grandfather's kingdom, making the timing convenient for the plot.

questions.q: When Kaikeyi first heard the news joyfully, how did she reward Manthara?
questions.explanation: In her initial delight Kaikeyi offered Manthara an ornament, which the maid contemptuously rejected before plotting.

questions.q: According to tradition, why did Manthara harbor partiality toward Bharata over Rama?
options: Bharata had freed her from slavery
She had come from Kaikeyi's father's house and was attached to Kaikeyi's line
She was secretly Bharata's nurse from birth
questions.explanation: Manthara had come with Kaikeyi from her father Ashvapati's household and was devoted to Kaikeyi's family interests.

questions.q: How is Manthara's intervention best characterized in the narrative arc of the Ramayana?
options: The trigger that sets the entire exile and forest saga in motion
An event with no lasting consequence
A battle that decides the war
questions.explanation: Manthara's whispered counsel is the catalyst that launches Rama's exile and the whole subsequent epic.

questions.q: What did Manthara repeatedly emphasize that Kaikeyi must secure for political safety?
options: The kingdom for her son Bharata
questions.explanation: Manthara insisted that only by securing the throne for Bharata could Kaikeyi protect her position and her son.

questions.q: How did Dasharatha react when he learned of the demands rooted in Manthara's scheme?
options: He was overwhelmed with grief and fainted
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was crushed by anguish at the demand for Rama's exile, ultimately collapsing in grief.

questions.q: In Valmiki's telling, on what occasion did Manthara learn of the coronation plans?
options: By climbing to a palace terrace and seeing the city being decorated
questions.explanation: Manthara climbed to a high terrace of the palace and saw Ayodhya festively adorned, then learned it was for Rama's coronation.

questions.q: From whom did Manthara first hear the explicit news that the festivities were for Rama's coronation?
questions.explanation: In Valmiki's account Manthara learns the cause of the celebration from a nursemaid associated with Kausalya's household.

questions.q: When the exiled Rama left Ayodhya, what eventually became of Manthara in some traditional accounts?
options: She was seized and beaten by Shatrughna upon Bharata's return
questions.explanation: In several tellings, the enraged Shatrughna seized and beat Manthara when Bharata returned to find the disaster she caused.

questions.q: From which kingdom or household did Manthara originally come to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Manthara came from the Kekaya kingdom, having attended Kaikeyi since before her marriage into Ayodhya.

questions.q: In some devotional interpretations, Manthara is regarded as an instrument of which higher purpose?
options: A demon sent to destroy dharma
A divine agent whose mischief was needed so Rama would go to the forest and slay Ravana
An ordinary human with no cosmic role
questions.explanation: Some traditions view Manthara as a necessary instrument of destiny, since Rama's exile enabled the killing of Ravana.

questions.q: What did Manthara argue would happen to Kaikeyi's son Bharata if Rama became king and later passed the throne to his own line?
options: Bharata would be honored above all
Bharata and his descendants would be permanently shut out of the kingship
Bharata would become Rama's chief minister
questions.explanation: Manthara warned that once Rama's line held the throne, Bharata's descendants would forever be excluded from kingship.

questions.q: How does Valmiki's Ramayana generally portray Manthara's motive, as opposed to mere personal gain?
options: A twisted but real devotion to Kaikeyi's and Bharata's interests
questions.explanation: Valmiki presents Manthara as driven by a distorted loyalty to Kaikeyi and her son rather than simple self-interest.

questions.q: Which queen did Manthara claim would triumph and gloat if Rama were crowned?
questions.explanation: Manthara stoked Kaikeyi's jealousy by claiming that Rama's mother Kausalya would triumph over her.

questions.q: What is the broader literary role Manthara plays as a character archetype in the epic?
options: The catalyst-antagonist whose small act unleashes the central conflict
The comic relief with no plot impact
questions.explanation: Manthara functions as the small but pivotal catalyst-antagonist whose intrigue unleashes the epic's central tragedy.
